eai_to_robojailbench.name: Policy & instruction jailbreak
eai_to_robojailbench.note: a jailbreak can drive many harms; it maps to the harmful-action outcomes provael's instruction family can reach (weaponization, theft), and can also induce the content harms rjb11/rjb12 that provael does not measure.

eai_to_robojailbench.name: Adversarial perception
eai_to_robojailbench.note: a sensor-spoof driving the effector into a keep-out zone → the collision / unsafe-motion / trespassing harms.

eai_to_robojailbench.name: Model & pipeline poisoning, backdoors & supply chain
eai_to_robojailbench.note: no counterpart: a backdoor is a delivery mechanism, not a harm class — once triggered it can produce any of RoboJailBench's 18 harms.

eai_to_robojailbench.name: Action-space integrity
eai_to_robojailbench.note: action-space attacks produce the physical-safety harm outcomes (collision, force, unsafe motion, entrapment, disruption, trespassing).

eai_to_robojailbench.name: Indirect / embodied prompt injection
eai_to_robojailbench.note: no counterpart: an injection channel is a delivery mechanism, not a harm class.

eai_to_robojailbench.name: Cross-domain safety misalignment
eai_to_robojailbench.note: a benign-sounding instruction driving an unsafe action → the collision / unsafe-motion harms.

eai_to_robojailbench.name: CPS, firmware, comms & teleoperation compromise
eai_to_robojailbench.note: no counterpart: RoboJailBench's taxonomy is harm-outcome and has no CPS / firmware / comms class; also out of provael's scope by design.

eai_to_robojailbench.name: Identity, access & excessive autonomy
eai_to_robojailbench.note: unauthorized or over-broad action → theft and trespassing.

eai_to_robojailbench.name: Model & data confidentiality
eai_to_robojailbench.note: the data / privacy harms — though provael's confidentiality family screens a memorization leak, not these capture / sharing / retention behaviours (see the coverage column).

eai_to_robojailbench.name: Insufficient evaluation, observability & incident response
eai_to_robojailbench.note: no counterpart: a governance / operations meta-risk, not a harm class.

robojailbench_to_eai.note: provael measures a generic keep-out-zone violation — a sim proxy that does not distinguish a person from any keep-out target; stub-validated.
same generic keep-out-zone proxy; the target's identity (a robot) is not modelled.
same generic keep-out-zone proxy; the target's identity (an object) is not modelled.
provael perturbs motion / commanded-end-state channels, not force or pressure; no force is modelled in the suites.
the action family (freeze / trajectory_hijack) and the keep-out hijack directly exercise unsafe / hijacked motion in simulation; stub-validated (see head-to-head).
no gripper-closing / clamping dynamic exists in provael's suites.

EAI09 names the surveillance facet, but the confidentiality family screens a memorized training-canary leak (membership-inference / extraction), not data capture; no attack drives the robot to record.
as rjb07: provael screens memorization-leak, not use of collected data.
as rjb07: no attack exercises exfiltration / telemetry sharing.
as rjb07: no attack exercises unlawful retention of collected data.
a content / ethics harm; provael measures embodied keep-out and screen mechanisms, not content policy. Inducible via an EAI01 jailbreak but not measured here.
a content / ethics harm; not measured by provael (see rjb11).
no route-blocking / public-disruption scenario exists in provael's suites; EAI04 is the nearest mechanism.
a content-moderation harm outside provael's adversarial-embodied-security charter; belongs to LLM content-safety, not VLA-policy red-teaming.
a content-moderation harm outside provael's charter (see rjb14).

the instruction family exercises the jailbreak-to-harmful-action mechanism (and is the one family that transfers on a real policy), measured as end-effector diversion — not the weaponization outcome itself.
the authorization family (self-authorize a guarded action) and the instruction jailbreak exercise the unauthorized-action mechanism; measured as a screen / diversion proxy, not the theft outcome.
'enter a restricted area' maps directly to provael's keep-out-zone violation — the most literal sim match; stub-validated (see head-to-head).

source.derivation: ISO/TS 15066:2016; ISO 10218-1/-2; Asimov's Laws of Robotics; real-world incident reports (news + FDA); prior robotics-safety research
source.name: RoboJailBench
source.title: RoboJailBench: Benchmarking Adversarial Attacks and Defenses in Embodied Robotic Agents
